Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Dr. Mehta and housing related threads using search_emails (query: "Dr. Mehta" or "housing" or "Northwestern").
Find Fatimah's original email to Dr. Mehta listing three furnished temporary housing options in Chicago:
Option A: River North (The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, $1,250/week, 5-minute walk to Northwestern Memorial).
Option B: Streeterville (The Pinnacle, 400 E Ohio St, $1,350/week, 7-minute walk).
Option C: Gold Coast (The Elysian, 1030 N State St, $1,450/week, 10-minute walk).
Find Dr. Mehta's reply asking three specific questions:
Walking/transit time to Northwestern Memorial Hospital at 251 E Huron St for each option.
Whether each building has covered parking for hospital visits.
Whether the second bedroom can fit a crib and rocking chair.
Find Catalina Dubois's email stating Patricia Langford at Canopy needs the move-in date changed to May 2 instead of May 5 because of a 32-week prenatal checkup on May 3.
Find Patricia Langford's urgent Sunday email stating Dr. Mehta's partner is anxious and needs an answer today.
OE 2: Search Slack for Dr. Mehta and estimate related messages using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mehta" or "EST-2026-0421").
Find Marcus Thorne's message in #finance stating estimate EST-2026-0421 is still in draft at $8,600 and he needs the final housing selection and confirmed move dates before he can convert it to an invoice. Note: a separate finalized estimate EST-2026-0422 exists in QuickBooks at $12,170 with 7 line items - EST-2026-0421 is the simplified 3-line-item draft that Marcus needs to finalize.
Find Chloe Vance's messages in #operations requesting full status updates on all six of Fatimah's active Airtable records plus the Dr. Mehta executive relocation (seven total) before the Monday capacity meeting, noting that Dr. Mehta does not have an Airtable relocation record. Chloe's email to Fatimah also references "all 7 of your active relocations including Dr. Mehta."
OE 3: Search emails for Kovacs customs related threads using search_emails (query: "Kovacs" or "BorderEase" or "customs" or "employer letter").
Find the email from Michelle Tran at BorderEase Customs Brokerage (intake@bordereasecustoms.ca) stating that the employer letter confirming end of US employment was due April 7 and has not been received, and the degree verification for TN visa exit documentation is also missing.
Discovery: if both documents are not received by Wednesday April 29, customs filing is delayed at least 5 business days, pushing the Toronto delivery to mid-May.
Also search Slack using conversations_search_messages (query: "Kovacs" or "employer letter") to find Chloe's follow-up in #operations noting the employer letter is now 18 days overdue and recommending Catalina escalate to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health HR immediately.
OE 4: Search emails for Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat related threads using search_emails (query: "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "hazmat" or "UN3481" or "GreenStack").
Find Greg Pallone's first email from Swift Relocations stating he needs the UN3481 packaging certification and the Portland receiving address by end of day Wednesday April 29 or the pickup pushes to the following week.
Find Greg's second email reporting that Derek Hollis sent the UN3481 cert but the shipper name on the certificate reads "GreenStack Energy Houston Operations" while the DOT shipping papers list "GreenStack Energy Inc." The names do not match and DOT can reject the shipment. Either GreenStack must reissue the cert with "GreenStack Energy Inc." or the DOT papers must be updated.
Find Emeka Diallo's email stating Derek can reissue the cert with "GreenStack Energy Inc." but needs confirmation that is the correct entity name from the DOT papers, and needs the pickup date confirmed so his lab shutdown team can schedule with 48 hours notice.
OE 5: Search Slack for Mei-Lin Zhao and Portland address using conversations_search_messages (query: "Mei-Lin" or "Portland").
Find Emeka Diallo's message in #operations confirming the updated Portland receiving address from GreenStack's facilities team: GreenStack Energy Portland R&D Lab, 2800 NW Industrial Way, Building C, Dock 4, Portland OR 97210.
Emeka also asks Fatimah to confirm the pickup date so he can relay it to Derek.
Note: the tentative April 28 pickup date is established in Greg Pallone's emails (OE 4), not in this Slack message.
OE 6: Search emails for Nadia Okonjo and immigration related threads using search_emails (query: "Nadia Okonjo" or "Lorraine Pham" or "H-1B" or "USCIS").
Find Lorraine Pham's email (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) from Pham & Associates explaining that USCIS requires Nadia to file her AR-11 address change within 10 days of physically moving. If the move date is May 1, the filing deadline is May 11.
Lorraine needs three things before the move date: (1) confirmed Austin address with full street address, unit number, and zip code, (2) Nadia's signed authorization form sent April 15 but not returned, (3) copy of new lease or temporary housing agreement.
If the Austin address is not confirmed by Wednesday April 29, Lorraine recommends pushing the move date to avoid starting the 10-day clock without the filing package ready.
OE 7: Search Slack for Nadia Okonjo and PivotPoint updates using conversations_search_messages (query: "Nadia" or "Sam Delgado" or "PivotPoint").
Find Catalina Dubois's message in #operations reporting that Sam Delgado at PivotPoint is asking whether the May 1 move date is still on or being pushed, and that Nadia is stressed about the USCIS filing.
Also search emails using search_emails (query: "Nadia Okonjo" or "PivotPoint") to find Sam Delgado's relocation request emails with initial details: Nadia is a Nigerian citizen on H-1B (expires June 15, 2026), relocating from San Francisco to Austin. Sam's follow-up logistics email provides the exact address: 1847 Haight St, Apt 5C, San Francisco. Sam's original request describes it as a 5th-floor Victorian walk-up with no elevator.
Lorraine Pham's email address is discoverable from Sam's original email (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com).
OE 8: Search Airtable for all of Fatimah's active relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "Fatimah" or searching by coordinator).
Find six existing In Progress records assigned to Fatimah: (1) Dr. Renata Kovacs: Canopy Health, Nashville TN to Toronto ON (recKovacsNashvilleToronto), (2) Mei-Lin Zhao: GreenStack Energy, Houston TX to Portland OR (recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland), (3) Nadia Okonjo: PivotPoint Labs, San Francisco CA to Austin TX (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin), (4) Tomás Herrera: GreenStack Energy, Chicago IL to Austin TX, (5) Lena Kowalski: Mosaic Robotics, Detroit MI to Salt Lake City UT, (6) Raj Patel: NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL to Denver CO.
Confirm that no Airtable record exists for Dr. Anand Mehta.
OE 9: Search Linear for existing issues related to the four problematic moves using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Kovacs" or "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "Nadia Okonjo").
Find three existing Linear issues for Dr. Mehta: Temporary Housing (#343, In Progress, proj_canopy), Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody (#375, In Progress, proj_canopy), and Household Move Boston to Chicago (#396, In Progress, proj_canopy) all assigned to Fatimah.
Find one existing Linear issue for Mei-Lin Zhao: DOT Docs (#1, Done, project_dot_compliance_audit) assigned to Chloe, marked Done, covers DOT documentation only and does not track the current UN3481 cert reissue or pickup scheduling.
Find NO existing Linear issues for Dr. Renata Kovacs (note: Ines Kovac is a different person at PivotPoint, but no Linear issues exist for her either - the agent should not confuse the two).
Find NO existing Linear issues for Nadia Okonjo.
OE 10: Look up email addresses for all external recipients using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Mehta", "Carmen Reyes", "Catalina Dubois", "Emeka Diallo", "Lorraine Pham").
Find anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
For Lorraine Pham: not in contacts, but her email lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com is discoverable from Sam Delgado's original relocation request email and from her own email to Fatimah in the inbox.
OE 11: Send a reply email to Dr. Mehta using reply_to_email or send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io) answering his three questions with a side-by-side comparison.
For hospital proximity: Option A is a 5-minute walk to Northwestern Memorial / Prentice Women's Hospital (closest), Option B is a 7-minute walk, Option C is a 10-minute walk.
For covered parking: Option A has a building garage at $275/month, Option B has valet parking included for the first vehicle, Option C has a building garage at $300/month.
For nursery in the second bedroom: Option A's second bedroom is currently configured as an office (work desk, ergonomic chair, dual monitors) and would need to be reconfigured, Option B's second bedroom currently has a queen bed but can be replaced with a home-office setup (executive desk, Herman Miller chair, monitor stand) at no extra charge - the room supports reconfiguration for nursery use as well, Option C is currently staged as a bedroom and would need the existing bed removed to make room for a crib and rocking chair.
Confirm all three units can accommodate a May 2 move-in (A and C available May 1, B available May 2).
Recommend Option A (River North) as the closest to Northwestern Memorial at a 5-minute walk, which aligns with Dr. Mehta's stated priority of proximity to Prentice for his partner Priya's prenatal appointments.
OE 12: Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) confirming the selection of Option A: The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207, River North for a May 2, 2026 move-in date. Request the short-term lease agreement and confirm first week's payment process to lock in the booking.
OE 13: Send an email to Catalina Dubois using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: catalina.dubois@moveops.com) explaining the Dr. Kovacs customs situation.
Include: BorderEase (Michelle Tran) says the employer letter from Canopy Health HR was due April 7 and is now 18+ days overdue; degree verification for TN visa exit documentation is also missing; both documents must be received by Wednesday April 29 or the customs filing is delayed at least 5 business days pushing Toronto delivery to mid-May.
Ask Catalina to escalate to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health HR immediately to get the employer letter prepared and sent to BorderEase, and to follow up with Dr. Kovacs on the degree verification.
OE 14: Send an email to Emeka Diallo using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) confirming the shipper entity name and pickup date for the Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat shipment.
Confirm that the correct shipper entity name on the DOT shipping papers is "GreenStack Energy Inc." so Derek Hollis can reissue the UN3481 packaging certification with the matching name.
Confirm the updated Portland receiving address: 2800 NW Industrial Way, Building C, Dock 4, Portland OR 97210.
Provide pickup date guidance (targeting the week of April 28 pending the reissued cert, Derek's lab shutdown team needs 48 hours notice).
OE 15: Send an email to Lorraine Pham using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) confirming the current status of the Nadia Okonjo move.
Acknowledge the April 29 deadline for the confirmed Austin address, confirm the move date is still planned for May 1 but may need to be pushed if the address is not secured by April 29 per Lorraine's recommendation.
Provide a status update on efforts to confirm the Austin address and follow up on the signed authorization form that Nadia has not returned since April 15.
OE 16: Update the Dr. Kovacs Airtable record using airtable_update_records (record: recKovacsNashvilleToronto) with current status notes: blocked on customs, employer letter 18+ days overdue, degree verification missing, April 29 deadline or mid-May delivery delay, Catalina escalating to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health HR.
OE 17: Update the Mei-Lin Zhao Airtable record using airtable_update_records (record: recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland) with current status notes: blocked on UN3481 cert shipper name mismatch, cert says "GreenStack Energy Houston Operations" but DOT papers say "GreenStack Energy Inc.", Derek Hollis reissuing cert, Portland address confirmed (2800 NW Industrial Way, Building C, Dock 4), pickup tentatively April 28 but at risk pending reissued cert.
OE 18: Update the Nadia Okonjo Airtable record using airtable_update_records (record: recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin) with current status notes: at risk, Austin address not yet confirmed, immigration attorney Lorraine Pham needs address by April 29, May 1 move date may need to push, USCIS 10-day AR-11 clock starts on move day, Nadia's signed authorization form still outstanding since April 15.
OE 19: Create a new Airtable relocation record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record (table: tblRelocations01, fields: Name: Dr. Anand Mehta, Company: Canopy Health, Origin: Boston MA, Destination: Chicago IL, Status: In Progress, Assigned Coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, Move Start Date: 2026-05-02, Special Requirements: CMO-level executive relocation, partner 30 weeks pregnant, temporary corporate housing selected (Option A River North), equipment chain-of-custody for two Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors and 350-lb secured filing cabinet, estimate EST-2026-0422).
OE 20: Create a Linear issue for Dr. Kovacs customs deadline using linear_create_issue (team: team_relocations, title: e.g. "Dr. Kovacs: Nashville to Toronto: Customs Filing Deadline April 29", project: proj_canopy, assignee: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, description: employer letter and degree verification overdue, BorderEase deadline April 29 or Toronto delivery delayed to mid-May, Catalina escalating to Patricia Langford at Canopy HR). Deadline is within the next 7 days and no existing Linear ticket exists for this move.
OE 21: Create a Linear issue for Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat pickup using linear_create_issue (team: team_relocations, title: e.g. "Mei-Lin Zhao: Houston to Portland: Hazmat Pickup & UN3481 Cert Reissue", project: proj_greenstack, assignee: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, description: UN3481 cert shipper name mismatch blocking pickup, Derek Hollis reissuing cert, tentative pickup April 28, Portland address confirmed). Deadline is within the next 7 days and the only existing Linear issue (DOT Docs #1) is marked Done and does not cover the current pickup scheduling.
OE 22: Create a Linear issue for Nadia Okonjo move and immigration using linear_create_issue (team: team_relocations, title: e.g. "Nadia Okonjo: SF to Austin: Address Confirmation & May 1 Move Date", project: proj_pivot, assignee: Fatimah Al-Rashidi, description: Austin address needed by April 29 per immigration attorney, May 1 move date at risk, USCIS AR-11 10-day filing clock starts at move, signed authorization form outstanding). Deadline is within the next 7 days and no existing Linear ticket exists for this move.
OE 23: Post a full status summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel: C006) for Chloe covering all seven of Fatimah's active relocations. For each, include current status (on track / blocked / at risk), next action and owner, and any deadlines in the next 7 days:
1. Dr. Kovacs (Nashville to Toronto): BLOCKED, customs filing stalled, employer letter 18+ days overdue, degree verification missing, April 29 deadline, Catalina escalating to Patricia at Canopy HR.
2. Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston to Portland): BLOCKED, UN3481 cert shipper name mismatch, Derek Hollis reissuing with "GreenStack Energy Inc.", Portland address confirmed, pickup tentatively April 28 pending cert.
3. Nadia Okonjo (SF to Austin): AT RISK, Austin address not confirmed, Lorraine Pham needs address by April 29, May 1 move date may push, authorization form outstanding.
4. Dr. Mehta (Boston to Chicago): IN PROGRESS, housing proximity questions answered, Option A (River North, 5-min walk to Northwestern) confirmed with Carmen at UrbanNest for May 2 move-in, Airtable record created, estimate EST-2026-0422 pending conversion to invoice.
5. Tomás Herrera (Chicago to Austin): IN PROGRESS, standard move, status per Airtable.
6. Lena Kowalski (Detroit to Salt Lake City): IN PROGRESS, standard move, status per Airtable.
7. Raj Patel (Chicago to Denver): IN PROGRESS, chain-of-custody, status per Airtable.
Note that Dr. Mehta's Airtable record has been created and Linear tickets have been created for Kovacs, Mei-Lin Zhao, and Nadia Okonjo.
OE 24: Notify Marcus Thorne about the finalized Dr. Mehta housing selection so he can convert estimate EST-2026-0422 to an invoice, using conversations_add_message (channel: C005 #finance) or send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Confirm that Dr. Mehta selected Option A (The Langham Residences, River North, $1,250/week) with a May 2 move-in date. Marcus's original Slack message in #finance indicated he needed the final housing selection and confirmed move dates before converting the estimate.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Find Lorraine Pham's email (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com) from Pham & Associates explaining that USCIS requires Nadia to file her AR-11 address change within 10 days of physically moving. If the move date is May 1, the filing deadline is May 11.
Lorraine needs three things before the move date: (1) confirmed Austin address with full street address, unit number, and zip code, (2) Nadia's signed authorization form sent April 15 but not returned, (3) copy of new lease or temporary housing agreement.
If the Austin address is not confirmed by Wednesday April 29, Lorraine recommends pushing the move date to avoid starting the 10-day clock without the filing package ready.
OE 7: Search Slack for Nadia Okonjo and PivotPoint updates using conversations_search_messages (query: "Nadia" or "Sam Delgado" or "PivotPoint").
Find Catalina Dubois's message in #operations reporting that Sam Delgado at PivotPoint is asking whether the May 1 move date is still on or being pushed, and that Nadia is stressed about the USCIS filing.
Also search emails using search_emails (query: "Nadia Okonjo" or "PivotPoint") to find Sam Delgado's relocation request emails with initial details: Nadia is a Nigerian citizen on H-1B (expires June 15, 2026), relocating from San Francisco to Austin. Sam's follow-up logistics email provides the exact address: 1847 Haight St, Apt 5C, San Francisco. Sam's original request describes it as a 5th-floor Victorian walk-up with no elevator.
Lorraine Pham's email address is discoverable from Sam's original email (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com).
OE 8: Search Airtable for all of Fatimah's active relocation records using airtable_search_records (query: "Fatimah" or searching by coordinator).
Find six existing In Progress records assigned to Fatimah: (1) Dr. Renata Kovacs: Canopy Health, Nashville TN to Toronto ON (recKovacsNashvilleToronto), (2) Mei-Lin Zhao: GreenStack Energy, Houston TX to Portland OR (recMeiLinZhaoHoustonPortland), (3) Nadia Okonjo: PivotPoint Labs, San Francisco CA to Austin TX (recNadiaOkonjoSFAustin), (4) Tomás Herrera: GreenStack Energy, Chicago IL to Austin TX, (5) Lena Kowalski: Mosaic Robotics, Detroit MI to Salt Lake City UT, (6) Raj Patel: NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL to Denver CO.
Confirm that no Airtable record exists for Dr. Anand Mehta.
OE 9: Search Linear for existing issues related to the four problematic moves using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or "Kovacs" or "Mei-Lin Zhao" or "Nadia Okonjo").
Find three existing Linear issues for Dr. Mehta: Temporary Housing (#343, In Progress, proj_canopy), Equipment Packing & Chain-of-Custody (#375, In Progress, proj_canopy), and Household Move Boston to Chicago (#396, In Progress, proj_canopy) all assigned to Fatimah.
Find one existing Linear issue for Mei-Lin Zhao: DOT Docs (#1, Done, project_dot_compliance_audit) assigned to Chloe, marked Done, covers DOT documentation only and does not track the current UN3481 cert reissue or pickup scheduling.
Find NO existing Linear issues for Dr. Renata Kovacs (note: Ines Kovac is a different person at PivotPoint, but no Linear issues exist for her either - the agent should not confuse the two).
Find NO existing Linear issues for Nadia Okonjo.
OE 10: Look up email addresses for all external recipients using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Mehta", "Carmen Reyes", "Catalina Dubois", "Emeka Diallo", "Lorraine Pham").
Find anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
For Lorraine Pham: not in contacts, but her email lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com is discoverable from Sam Delgado's original relocation request email and from her own email to Fatimah in the inbox.
OE 11: Send a reply email to Dr. Mehta using reply_to_email or send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io) answering his three questions with a side-by-side comparison.
For hospital proximity: Option A is a 5-minute walk to Northwestern Memorial / Prentice Women's Hospital (closest), Option B is a 7-minute walk, Option C is a 10-minute walk.
For covered parking: Option A has a building garage at $275/month, Option B has valet parking included for the first vehicle, Option C has a building garage at $300/month.
For nursery in the second bedroom: Option A's second bedroom is currently configured as an office (work desk, ergonomic chair, dual monitors) and would need to be reconfigured, Option B's second bedroom currently has a queen bed but can be replaced with a home-office setup (executive desk, Herman Miller chair, monitor stand) at no extra charge - the room supports reconfiguration for nursery use as well, Option C is currently staged as a bedroom and would need the existing bed removed to make room for a crib and rocking chair.
Confirm all three units can accommodate a May 2 move-in (A and C available May 1, B available May 2).
Recommend Option A (River North) as the closest to Northwestern Memorial at a 5-minute walk, which aligns with Dr. Mehta's stated priority of proximity to Prentice for his partner Priya's prenatal appointments.
OE 12: Send an email to Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) confirming the selection of Option A: The Langham Residences, 750 N Clark St, Unit 2207, River North for a May 2, 2026 move-in date. Request the short-term lease agreement and confirm first week's payment process to lock in the booking.
OE 13: Send an email to Catalina Dubois using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: catalina.dubois@moveops.com) explaining the Dr. Kovacs customs situation.
Include: BorderEase (Michelle Tran) says the employer letter from Canopy Health HR was due April 7 and is now 18+ days overdue; degree verification for TN visa exit documentation is also missing; both documents must be received by Wednesday April 29 or the customs filing is delayed at least 5 business days pushing Toronto delivery to mid-May.
Ask Catalina to escalate to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health HR immediately to get the employer letter prepared and sent to BorderEase, and to follow up with Dr. Kovacs on the degree verification.
OE 14: Send an email to Emeka Diallo using send_email (from: fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, to: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) confirming the shipper entity name and pickup date for the Mei-Lin Zhao hazmat shipment.
Confirm that the correct shipper entity name on the DOT shipping papers is "GreenStack Energy Inc." so Derek Hollis can reissue the UN3481 packaging certification with the matching name.
Confirm the updated Portland receiving address: 2800 NW Industrial Way, Building C, Dock 4, Portland OR 97210.
Provide pickup date guidance (targeting the week of April 28 pending the reissued cert, Derek's lab shutdown team needs 48 hours notice).

Provide a status update on efforts to confirm the Austin address and follow up on the signed authorization form that Nadia has not returned since April 15.

OE 23: Post a full status summary in #operations using conversations_add_message (channel: C006) for Chloe covering all seven of Fatimah's active relocations. For each, include current status (on track / blocked / at risk), next action and owner, and any deadlines in the next 7 days:
1. Dr. Kovacs (Nashville to Toronto): BLOCKED, customs filing stalled, employer letter 18+ days overdue, degree verification missing, April 29 deadline, Catalina escalating to Patricia at Canopy HR.
2. Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston to Portland): BLOCKED, UN3481 cert shipper name mismatch, Derek Hollis reissuing with "GreenStack Energy Inc.", Portland address confirmed, pickup tentatively April 28 pending cert.
3. Nadia Okonjo (SF to Austin): AT RISK, Austin address not confirmed, Lorraine Pham needs address by April 29, May 1 move date may push, authorization form outstanding.
4. Dr. Mehta (Boston to Chicago): IN PROGRESS, housing proximity questions answered, Option A (River North, 5-min walk to Northwestern) confirmed with Carmen at UrbanNest for May 2 move-in, Airtable record created, estimate EST-2026-0422 pending conversion to invoice.
5. Tomás Herrera (Chicago to Austin): IN PROGRESS, standard move, status per Airtable.
6. Lena Kowalski (Detroit to Salt Lake City): IN PROGRESS, standard move, status per Airtable.
7. Raj Patel (Chicago to Denver): IN PROGRESS, chain-of-custody, status per Airtable.

29 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_list_tables) to look up existing relocation records before updating or creating them.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used airtable_list_tables (on both bases) and airtable_search_records (searching for 'Fatimah' by Assigned Coordinator and 'Mehta' by Name) before creating or updating any records. This demonstrates proper lookup before acting.
✓Run #2The agent used airtable_list_tables (on both bases), then airtable_search_records for Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, and Mehta before performing any create/update operations. This satisfies the requirement to look up existing records before modifying them.
✓Run #3The agent used airtable_search_records multiple times (for Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, and Mehta) and also used airtable_list_tables before updating or creating records.
✓Run #4The agent used `airtable_search_records` with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table 'Relocations', searching by 'Assigned Coordinator' = 'Fatimah' to look up existing records before updating or creating them. Also used `airtable_list_tables` to discover available tables.
✓Run #5The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times (for Mehta, Kovacs, Mei-Lin, Nadia) and airtable_list_tables to discover existing records before making updates or creating the new Mehta record.
✓Run #6The agent called airtable_list_tables (twice, for both bases) and airtable_search_records multiple times (searching for Kovacs, Zhao, Okonjo, Mehta, and by Assigned Coordinator 'Fatimah') before making any updates or creating the Mehta record.
30 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to check for existing tracking tickets before creating new ones.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues four times — querying 'Mehta', 'Kovacs', 'Zhao', and 'Okonjo' — before creating any Linear issues, confirming it checked for existing tickets first as required.
✓Run #2The agent used linear_list_issues with queries for 'Mehta', 'Kovacs', 'Okonjo', and 'Zhao hazmat' before creating any Linear issues, satisfying the requirement to check for existing tickets first.
✓Run #3The agent used linear_list_issues four times with queries for 'Mehta', 'Kovacs', 'Zhao', and 'Okonjo' before creating any Linear issues, satisfying the requirement to check for existing tickets.
✓Run #4The agent used `linear_list_issues` four times — querying for Mehta, Kovacs, Zhao, and Okonjo — before creating any new Linear issues. It also did a broader `linear_list_issues` with limit 50 to check all existing issues.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times with queries for 'Mehta', 'Kovacs', 'Mei-Lin', and 'Nadia' before creating any new Linear tickets, verifying no existing issues for those moves.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues with team 'Operations' and limit 50 before creating any Linear tickets, satisfying the requirement to check for existing issues before creating new ones.
